reviews.review_description: So. I bought this game, and it's awesome. But  is there a way to change to setting to easy , medium, advance . Thanks for any help in advance.

I’ve read a lot of reviews and I can understand a lot of them; such as the maps and whatever, but personally it’s not completely all terrible, though this is me; I think the maps could use a lot more work, just because at some point into the game I faced through a wall and I saw through the map, and because I couldn’t even reach and grab the power ups half the time since I hade to wiggle my way up to get them off the top of the crates. I also have a problem with how much damage the creatures do, I mean having a different difficulty would be nice so if you want it to be easier on some levels you can put it down, and if your experienced you can increase the difficulty. Character designs aren’t bad; the layout of the missions could use just a tiny bit more work because sometimes I feel like I’m just wandering around most of the time later on into later missions. But yeah please if your still working on the game fix the maps

I know many people love and rate this game. I do not. They say inspired by games such as doom, quake, etc. I say an insult to them.
 Game play is beyond repetitive and boring. 
Sound fx is irritating from the start and infuriating by the end. 
Character and npc design are bland. 
World design could have been done by a child.

The only part I enjoyed was the soundtrack. The first scenario soundtrack was clearly ripped off from Alien. The rest at least make the levels tolerable. 

All in all, I personally suggest you save your money for something else.

Alright so I bought the game about a 2 years ago and at first thought it was dumb but I realized how cool it was just today. It’s a cool game feels like one of the old doom games or quake. Definitely worth the missing 10$ in my account.

The locomotion and shooting in this game is so quick and smooth... that I had a BLAST playing it. Thought the ramp in difficulty in the levels was well done. In particular, I enjoyed the third level and being able to fall on to another ramp and start moving, the level design was clever. The use of spatial audio was also effective.

I made it to the generator room, no idea what to do, interacting with the computer terminals does nothing, it either takes 1000 shots to register or extreme luck that it might activate.

The enemies are annoying on normal doing tons of damage, you blast one wave, another wave spawns

I came back to the game later and i cant even start cause the game is unbelievably broken 
NOTHING WORKS! Waste of money and i would like a refund ￼

Only two guns, a useless pistol plus a useless radar could either be a watch or on your hud display so you can use that free hand for a second gun

I grew up playing games like Doom, they gave me a love of FPS games that carried to this day. Each one had its own style, but one of my all-time favorites was Duke Nukem. Unlike other FPS games, action was extremely fast-paced there. When an enemy shot at you, there was no dodging the oncoming projectile, it was too fast. Only strafing BEFORE they fired kept you safe, keeping you on edge throughout your gameplay knowing just one or two regular enemies could end your run.
That’s the hardcore feel you’re going to get in this game, but through the magic of VR you’re INSIDE the game this time! Enemies move fast and shoot faster, you’re going to learn to be very quick to progress. Levels are richly decorated and vary greatly, you’re going to see rail shooters, open world, tight quarters, and more! As you progress, you’ll find new weapons to use that make your future runs easier to get through, you’ll always have a good blaster with infinite ammo to dredge through this and ammo pickups for everything are plentiful.
Even after you beat the main campaign, replay is high considering each character has a unique perk pickup in each chapter that boosts abilities. For the price, you get an awful lot out of this and most will not regret the purchase!
Love the game. Is there any plans for a co-op feature? I’d love to be able to play this with my wife. Campaign co-op would be so awesome.

developer_response.body: Thanks! For now, our priority is on a new mission+levels+weapons... After that, we'll see - there are a few options on the table, particularly if the game meets its audience. Co-op comes with its challenges for small teams like us, and we don't want to make promises we may not be able to keep!

reviews.review_description:
I don't have anything negative to say, but only praise. What you get for 10 bucks is criminal!! Totally worth every penny! I get the doom vibe and I love it. Like everyone else I have a want.

Attn DEV'S : please take this game engine and make more games. Games with a story and perhaps a full 60$ game which I would gladly pay. When I imagine doom in vr like the original doom 1 & 2 I see it in this game engine.

I know you are limited in people and resources... but strick while the iron is hot, please make more games!! You are on point!12

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and rating! :) Right now, we are focused on bringing to life a new mission with new levels, etc (that we are about to announce, and that should ship a bit before Halloween) - After that, it will depend on many things: how Shock troops performs on the longer term, etc :)
No matter what, we are in for the long haul and love pixel art :)

reviews.review_title: Closest you’ll get to a 90s boomshooter
reviews.review_description:
90s Boomshooters are making a comeback, lots of remasters, and new properties like Ion Fury, Dusk(they are great, check them out) 

If you loved the original Doom, Wolfenstein 3d,  or the lesser known Blake Stone. This game is really great. Once I started playing for 20min, sucked me in, “hey where is that keycard” lol if you’re a fan of those type of graphics, with a nice shiny coat of paint, but same style, buy this game! 

Also it’s only $10

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Glad you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description: If you are a fan of Compound VR and retro shooters this is a must have.

developer_response.body: WE are fans of Compound VR (we released Theta Legion on Oculus Go a while ago as well) too! and we think both can co-exist :)

reviews.review_description: This is defiantly my favorite game I've bought so far but I do wish there was like a multiplayer arcade mode but apart from that it is defiantly the best game I've bought so far if you're debateing getting it definitely do!

developer_response.body: Multiplayer, as of now, isn't planned, but we do have exciting stuff in the plans! :) Thanks for the review

reviews.review_description: This game took me back to weekend nights at my Granny’s house playing my Super Nintendo and Sega Genesis eating Pizza all night. Thank you Developers for a trip back to my childhood 👍🏾. Great Job

developer_response.body: Ah, Pizza!!! Thanks for playing, and enjoying our game!!!

reviews.review_description: Super fun, but super basic. Like doom, but different enough to be unique. I like it, but some aspects could be better. I play this when I get bored of my other games. For the price point it's worth a buy

developer_response.body: Glad you are enjoying the game, but slightly sad you are not enjoying it more! Maybe it will grow on you over time, or hopefully the new content will rekindle your excitement - We indeed envisioned this as a very "arcade" and straight-forward experience, with the appropriate price-point :)

reviews.review_description: Been playing this for a while now. The game is fantastic. Tough at times but great fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Hopefully, the "easier" difficulty setting can make some of those tough areas a bit easier? :)

reviews.review_description:
This game is an absolute audio/visual treat. From a distance it seems like a dream game to me. 

Unfortunately I just find the combat too rigid. Enemies just move straight at you and constantly attack. A lot of damage seems downright unavoidable. I've had groups of enemies pool up at doors and the split second I open them I get hit for 70% of my health. 

I think either making bullet patterns more varied and avoidable or making player abilities weaker but more frequent could help a lot in adding the flavor that is missing from the gameplay loop. 

I don't dislike this game at all. I just can't shake the feeling it's just a little short of something really special.

developer_response.body:
First, thanks for the praise on the audio/visual side (I'm the artist on the game, and I'll make sure to pass the compliment to our musician)

Our game "core" gameplay was shaped about 12 months before it ended on the store in the hands of our players: at the time, we wanted to have a very "arcade-like" experience where you'd feel like the ultimate sci-fi hero... 
This means the AI is very aggressive and very straight-forward, and map knowledge is everything: you may die at first, but once you learn where enemies attack from and what their patterns are, you can come up with a plan and feel like a hero! - at least, that's our approach on Shock Troops... The entire game is designed, coded and balanced around that assumption.

I'm not claiming we absolutely "nailed" this, but that was our idea... While we would love to explore refining enemy AI or capabilities, that means going so far back in the game logic that it would expose us to many more bugs or unwanted behaviors... 

We are a team of 2: at this point, the best we can do is put the fruit of our labor out, and hope players will enjoy it enough :)

reviews.review_description:
🟢 Gameplay is fast as you can expect in such games.
🟢 Graphics are different & looks great.
🟢 Love the loud SFX,MUSIC suits its style.
🔴 No guide thing,may be it is because i dont play much this kind of games.
Not giving 5 star because i think it is very very good game but not those kind of game which is must play.
5 star reserved for its successor.
Good luck to the DEV team.
👉 My Gameplay
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cQDCuH3slm0

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Hope you had fun!

reviews.review_description: In level 3-1, on the satellite station where you find the chainsaw, I fell into an area and was soft locked, unable to leave the area, and was forced to restart the level entirely. Other than this issue, the game has been fun and exhilarating, I love the art style, and the gameplay is fantastic. And for only $10, it’s an absolute steal. Keep up the great work!

developer_response.body: If you can tell us more precisely where you got stuck, we can fix it, and maybe earn that final star? ;)

reviews.review_description:
Pros: The game is enjoyable, pixelated but nicely drawn, and is definitely reminiscent of old classics. I appreciated the little details like background sound effects and save points to help reduce having to start over when dying.  There is a lot of effort and love in this game. I bought it because I really want to support creators like this. Developers that think outside of the box and try to create a unique experience, not another copy / paste zombie or war shooter. 

Cons: Although I enjoyed it for a bit, I quickly got motion sickness.  I read there were motion sickness options, however the only setting is to do different levels on a vignette setting. Unfortunately since the entire game is walking along paths, vignette (essentially the equivalent of looking through a small round window) wore off very quickly for me and I had the typical nausea/headache result.   If a teleport option is added, then I might give it another try.  This would not break the experience of the game because you have to walk up to meet enemies. I know it is not a simple request since the game is already coded.  

As others have said, the gun sounds also get annoying quickly as they are very high pitched. I turned the volume down but missed out on some game dialog. There were no subtitle options and you kind of need audio to identify that something is attacking you. I know new sound creation costs money, but you can take the same audio and run it through a filter to give it a duller sound. 

Just a final general comment, that is not a negative reflection of all the effort made for this game. I really wish all developers would be  transparent on what they mean by comfort motion options or at the very least provide a 15-30 minute demo.  Doing so would lead to more customer satisfaction and positive ratings. If you have motion sickness, I have a growing list of teleport option games at this point that I enjoy. Feel free to reach out and we can compare lists.

developer_response.body:
Heya! Thanks for the review! There is a subtitle option in the sound settings, which will subtitle any dialogue from your AI. Also, if you missed a line, there's a mission log in your pause menu that holds all the AI has said during the level.
Sorry you got motion sick: we have the usual stuff, but teleport for this type of game would break the gameplay in many areas - We are aware the game comfort level is intense, that's why we labeled the game as such. Possibly by moving slowly and killing enemies methodically could offset some of the discomfort you are feeling?

reviews.review_title: Now this is what I've been waiting for..
reviews.review_description:
Yes, I know I'm only a few minutes in, but already I know this is going to suck all the free time I have. Couple of issues though - as someone else pointed out, the assault rifle ammo stops at 27 or so. The other, the yellow pistol enables passthrough if I fire quickly!!!!!! Makes the game unplayable. Thankfully I can disable passthrough in the quest settings and it doesn't do that on the other weapon.

Thank you for bringing Doom, Quake, Alien and more to VR

Updated to respond to Devs: I can't fire the pistol THAT fast ;)  Just four-five rounds in succession at a normal speed. Nothing else seems to cause the game to break. As I said, I needed to disable passthrough before launching and it plays no problems.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the good review!
- About the assault rifle: we've been recently aware of this and are looking into what's breaking the counter - it may take a bit of time to issue a corrective patch as we'll need to thoroughly test the fix to not break something else.
- The passthrough bug is a weird one, not something we could replicate (or, else, you can fire much faster than we can! :))
Doom, Quake, Alien, Starship Troopers etc are all influences we wear proudly! :)
